title: Cyprus Workshop Report: The Secondary Publication Right
content:
Blog post by Giorgos Glossiotis

On Monday, 16 March 2026, KR21 organised a workshop, with the support of the Cyprus Association of Librarians – Information Scientists (CALIS, ΚΕΒΕΠ), titled “The Secondary Publication Right: Concept, Legal Framework, and the Bulgarian Experience”, which was held in Nicosia, at the University of Cyprus Library. The event was led by Ana Lazarova, KR21 national coordinator for Bulgaria.

Following a warm welcome from CALIS President Theodora Petrou, participants were introduced to KR21’s new campaign, “Let Libraries be Libraries,”
creator: KR21 Guest
sourceDescription: We advocate for your right to access and share knowledge!

title: 14 April Webinar | Closed Licences, Closed Doors: Open Licensing, AI, and the Future of Research Impact
content:
When?

  • Date: 14 April 2026
  • Time: 11:00 – 12:30 CEST / 10:00 – 11:30 BST

What?

Open licensing and copyright are important aspects of publishing research, but what do they mean in practice? Creative Commons (CC) licences offer a standardised framework for sharing research outputs, with open access being increasingly mandated by international funders. However, growing concern about research outputs being used to train generative AI tools has led some authors to seek more restrictive licences. Yet the question remains: Does a more restrictive licence stop AI
creator: KR21
sourceDescription: We advocate for your right to access and share knowledge!

title: Portuguese Civil Society Proposes Secondary Publication Rights and Obligation
content:
Blog post by Eduardo Leonardo dos Santos

On 5 February, the magnificent Great Hall of the prestigious Academy of Sciences of Lisbon hosted Portugal’s first conference in Portugal on Secondary Publication Rights (SPR). Not even an extraordinary storm that struck Lisbon for several days could prevent an afternoon of lively discussion on the role legislation should play in advancing Open Access to publicly funded scientific outputs.

The event was organised by civil society organisations Knowledge Rights 21, COMMUNIA, and the Portuguese Association of Librarians, Archivists and Information and
creator: KR21 Guest
